final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial photo of emperor penguins marching across sea ice before a massive blue glacier near the Ross Ice Shelf, Antarctica, at polar midnight sun — National Geographic cover level.

Style/medium: photorealistic polar nature photography, ultra-wide-angle lens, color-accurate rendering in the spirit of National Geographic editorial photography
Composition: ultra-wide-angle shot, low horizon line, foreground close-up of 2–3 emperor penguins with the vast marching colony receding into the middle ground, towering blue glacier filling the background
Subject: hundreds of emperor penguins in a sweeping procession across sea ice; foreground individuals showing crisp black-and-white plumage, vivid orange-yellow beak detail, and a chick nestled on a parent's feet
Scene/backdrop: Ross Ice Shelf vicinity, Antarctica; a colossal blue glacier with layered textures ranging from pale ice-blue to deep cobalt, with mysterious blue light glowing from within crevasses; distant icebergs floating on a calm polar sea
Lighting/mood: polar midnight sun low on the horizon, casting pale gold light across the scene; warm golden sky tones contrasting with the cold blue glacier and ice — surreal, sublime, fragile
Color palette: deep cobalt and pale glacial blue as dominant tones, warm pale gold in the sky as counterpoint, black-and-white penguin forms as graphic anchors, orange-yellow beak accents
Materials/textures: visible individual feather strands on foreground penguins, translucent cracked ice surface with internal blue glow, matte compressed snow on sea ice, glassy calm water in the far distance
Avoid: artificial HDR glow, oversaturated colors, deformed penguin anatomy, studio lighting artifacts, generic stock-photo flatness
